examples.title: Calculate Risk Priority Number (RPN)
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate and categorize risk priority
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateRPN(probability, impact)
 // Input: probability (1-5), impact (1-5)
 // Output: RPN and priority category
 
 rpn = probability * impact
 
 IF rpn >= 15 THEN
 priority = "HIGH - Immediate action required"
 ELSE IF rpn >= 8 THEN
 priority = "MEDIUM - Plan mitigation soon"
 ELSE
 priority = "LOW - Monitor periodically"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN rpn, priority
END FUNCTION

// Example usage
rpnVal, category = CalculateRPN(4, 5)
OUTPUT "RPN: ", rpnVal, " Category: ", category
examples.explanation: Calculates RPN by multiplying probability and impact ratings, then categorizes into High/Medium/Low priority levels for risk response planning.

examples.title: Risk Exposure Calculation
examples.description: Calculate expected monetary value and risk exposure for multiple risks
examples.explanation: Calculates Expected Monetary Value (EMV) for each risk and determines the contingency budget needed to cover potential risk impacts.

examples.title: Risk Categorization and Response Selection
examples.description: Determine appropriate risk response strategy based on risk characteristics
examples.code:
FUNCTION DetermineRiskResponse(probability, impact, costToMitigate)
 // Determine RPN
 rpn = probability * impact
 potentialLoss = probability * impact * 10000 // Assume scale factor
 
 // Decision logic for response strategy
 IF rpn >= 15 AND costToMitigate < potentialLoss THEN
 strategy = "MITIGATION"
 reason = "High risk, mitigation cost-effective"
 ELSE IF rpn >= 12 AND costToMitigate >= potentialLoss THEN
 strategy = "TRANSFERENCE"
 reason = "High risk, transfer to third party"
 ELSE IF rpn < 5 THEN
 strategy = "ACCEPTANCE"
 reason = "Low priority risk, monitor only"
 ELSE
 strategy = "AVOIDANCE"
 reason = "Modify plan to eliminate risk"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN strategy, reason
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Uses RPN and cost-benefit analysis to automatically recommend the most appropriate risk response strategy for a given risk.
